andong.description: Andong hansik (traditional Andong-style Korean food spread), representing Andong regional kimchi tradition

buchukimchi.description: Buchu-geotjeori (fresh chive kimchi), a variety of buchu kimchi

changnan.description: Changnan-jeot (salted pollock innards), the base ingredient of changnanjeot kimchi

dalrae.description: Allium monanthum (dalrae/Korean wild chive), the main ingredient of dalrae kimchi

doraji.description: Korean kimchi dish, representing doraji (bellflower root) kimchi style

geoje.description: Geoje regional oyster cuisine, representing Geoje-style kimchi tradition

ggwarigochu.description: Kkwari-gochu (shishito/wrinkled green peppers), the main ingredient of ggwarigochu kimchi

gochu.description: Gochu-jangajji (pickled green peppers), closely related to gochu sobagi kimchi

goguma.description: Goguma-julgi-bokkeum (stir-fried sweet potato stems), representing goguma julgi kimchi ingredient

gulkimchi.description: Korean kimchi with jeotgal (fermented seafood), representing gul (oyster) kimchi style

hobak.description: Hobak-seon (stuffed zucchini/pumpkin), representing hobak (pumpkin) kimchi tradition

jangkimchi.description: Jangajji and namul at Namdaemun Market, representing jang kimchi (soy sauce-based kimchi) tradition

jeju.description: Jeju-do traditional hobakyeot (pumpkin taffy), representing Jeju regional food culture and kimchi

jeotgal.description: Various jeotgal (Korean fermented seafood) at Gyeongdong Market, key ingredient in jeotgal kimchi

jinju.description: Jinju bibimbap, iconic Jinju regional cuisine representing Jinju kimchi tradition

jogaejeotgal.description: Korean salted seafood (jeotgal), representing jogae (clam/shellfish) jeotgal kimchi

jokbal.description: Jokbal (Korean braised pig's feet), served alongside jokbal-mu kimchi
